item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an SCP that actually scared me deep in my soul and I commend you for that. The first person perspective of Elizabeth as she slowly looses her mind sent shivers down my spine. And there’s so many uncertainties, was Tony actually fake? what really happened to Elizabeth? did SCP-3890-2 really die? how did all of this happen? Quite the interesting read.

+1

(Sorry my review didn’t really say much I’m new to the wiki)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can’t tell you how hopeless that ending felt, so damn depressing.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: This was one of the best SCPs Ive ever read and there are so many questions I have. How were her notes discovered, what happened to her, did she actually disappear or did she get back or what? I love this +1. My only complaint is I can't +1 this more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A beautifully chilling little nightmare. I think maybe the first SCP to chill me this badly in years.

I have fibromyalgia; one of the symptoms of these are 'fibro fogs', which are basically a state of impacted cognition. An explanation I've read is that your brain isn't getting enough oxygen, which is fun. But the main thing is your memory just. Goes. Short term memory, long term memory.

You spend a lot of time worrying you're going to lose something important. Or you're going to lose something forever. Or this is going to be forever.

This really captured, I think, that same suffocating, terrified feeling. Extremely well done. Even personal investment aside, I really think this is a great example of doing creative things with this format.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first person aspect is pretty self-explanatory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remove the first person stuff unless there is a good reason and then I’ll upvote.

item.title: Best meta SCP ever!
item.content:encoded: I have finally found my favorite SCP! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's no way out of here, darling.

Chilling when you realize the context.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t have much to say other than Well Done.

Just very good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Iteration five appears to contradict itself slightly - Graham says "for which organisation?" but later refers to a Foundation special ops team.

Graham is wondering why SCP-3890-2 would be writing an SCP document - what SCP-esque organization could it possibly work for that could benefit from it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Iteration five appears to contradict itself slightly - Graham says "for which organisation?" but later refers to a Foundation special ops team. For a little while I thought that might be a hint that this would head in an unexpected direction.

As it is, I think the progression is a little too straightforward, and I echo shaggy's thoughts on Graham as a character. There is some decent writing in here, but nothing to really elevate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At the end of each iteration, there should be a word in red that takes you to the next one. Click on that! If I remember correctly, there are seven iterations in all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there only two of them?
We made it, fol…wait, what was I talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i even saw the last punch coming and said "oh wow" aloud as it landed this is exceptionally well done

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing that out, I'll just go and fix it now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic work! Hearty upvote!

One thing, on the second iteration, it says ''it knocked me unconsciousness''. I think that was supposed to be it ''knocked me into unconsciousness''.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How dare you make me tear up at work

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the colon on offset 7 meant to link to offset 7?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I enjoyed this a lot.

+ show block
– hide block

Excellent story, excellent pacing, great use of a format screw. I like to think that somehow, the Foundation -actually- has a way to record these documents as they're written.

At first, I thought that offset/4 was Elizabeth writing the document after her memories had been eaten, and it worked great as sort of an Alzheimer-stricken writing. It was truly horrifying from that angle. When I saw that it was the Monster, it became even more interesting, and I had to re-read it from that perspective.

Not sure if you are aware, but someone on r/SCP has already done some fan art of this:

https://www.reddit.com/r/SCP/comments/85ilvx/fanart_of_scp3890/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few people on r/SCP have been talking about doing an Erikeshan-themed SCP. AFAIK, the only mention of it -currently- is few hints in SCP-2317.

Maybe it'll be the next ancient civilization en vogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember it from SCP-2317.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Erikeshan

Should I recognize this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The execution of the format is pretty stellar and novel enough to appreciate, and the easter egg threading these is definitely a nice touch. I had a similar idea for an approach somewhat like this, so it's really cool to see something like it being received so well.

That being said, my downvote is primarily affected by what seems to me like an underdeveloped character in Gharam. Despite the amount of time we spend with the character, I don't think there's enough to really connect to, and it didn't go far enough to resonate with me on any level. I would have been much more satisfied had I been able to truly care for the character at any point.

Some amount of pathos and thematic coherency would go a long way to elevate this above being an impressive format screw, I feel. In its current state, it's pretty cool, but mostly underwhelming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There should be some text in red near the bottom of the page each iteration. Click that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I'm missing something here… how do I get it to advance iterations?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Come with me here, darling, and disappear." (commas and period added by me)

Is that -2 talking? Is that Graham losing her mind? Is that anything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a fan of the lack of justification for this to appear as an SCP document, or the yet another infinite bleak alternate dimension. However, the story was solid and tightly-written enough to offset (ha) these complaints. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And then it just loops? D: Oh no!

Part of me wonders why or how this would appear in-universe, but as format screws go, fuck it, it's golden. I really got into this, too. Just imagine me shouting, "Yeah! You kick its shit in, Dr. Graham!" I'm sorry she didn't get out. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn that twist hurt. +1.

item.title: You Absolute Mad Man
item.content:encoded: See post title

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no words. Great story hook. +1 Loved how you used the format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely phenomenal. +1 with glee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did not know who he was fuckin' with.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Westrin, PhamtomGuy, CadaverCommander, CaptainKirby, NineVolt, Stallmantic, not_a_seagull, mdvs and XilasCrowe.

At the moment, I am having some difficulties getting the iterations set up - it's showing the second one instead of the first. I would really appreciate if you'd refrain from reading before I finish setting it up!

And there we go! Technical difficulties over!
